title: hieracium lachenalii subsp. haematochromum
intro: Hieracium lachenalii subsp. haematochromum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Asia. It is also known as the blood-red hawkweed, a perennial herb with medicinal properties that have been used for centuries to treat various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, due to its high content of flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds. The leaves and roots of Hieracium lachenalii subsp. haematochromum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and have been used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
